name: KeyCloneMatic

consumes.minimum: 1 active comp + 1 selected layer with at least 1 selected keyframe on a time-varying property
consumes.description: Reads app.project.activeItem (must be a CompItem), iterates comp.selectedLayers, then each layer's selectedProperties, then iterates all keyframes checking keySelected(i). Captures keyTime, keyValue, interpolation types, temporal ease, spatial tangents, label colour, and roving state for each selected keyframe.

controls.name: Interval Duration (frames)
controls.role: sets the number of frames between successive clone sets

controls.name: No. of Repetitions
controls.role: sets how many times the selected keyframe block is cloned (ignored when Comp Duration is checked)

controls.name: Comp Duration
controls.role: auto-calculates repetitions to fill the remaining composition duration

controls.name: Temporal Decay
controls.role: enables progressive interval shortening across repetitions using the selected decay curve

controls.name: Decay Duration (frames)
controls.role: sets the total frame span over which the temporal decay effect is applied

controls.name: Decay Type
controls.role: selects the easing curve (Linear, Ease In, or Ease Out) used to calculate per-repetition decay factors

controls.name: Cancel
controls.role: closes the dialog without performing any operation

controls.name: Proceed
controls.role: validates inputs and triggers keyframe duplication

functions: collect all selected keyframes from all selected layers and their selected properties
detect the global in-point and out-point of the selected keyframe block
calculate per-repetition interval with optional linear, ease-in, or ease-out temporal decay
clone each selected keyframe to a new time offset, preserving value, interpolation type, temporal ease, spatial tangents, roving flag, and label colour
auto-calculate repetition count to fill remaining comp duration when Comp Duration is enabled
guard against missing active composition and missing keyframe selection with user alerts
wrap all operations in a single undo group for safe reversal

scriptSim.scenario: success — duplicate 2 keyframes x3 reps at 24f interval (linear, no decay)
scriptSim.inputs: Main Comp (1920x1080, 20 s, 24 fps) — Shape Layer 1 selected; Transform/Position property selected with 2 keyframes at t=0 s ([100,540]) and t=1 s ([500,540]); intervalInput=24, repetitionsInput=3, no comp duration, no temporal decay

notes: The script calls app.beginUndoGroup() and app.endUndoGroup() unconditionally at the top-level script scope, so both calls appear even when guard alerts abort the duplication.
Keyframe cloning uses property.addKey(newTime) + setValueAtKey(), so the harness logs these as 'addKeyframe' + 'setValue' pairs, not 'setValueAtTime'.
The ease() helper in the source is a simple linear interpolation (c*t/d + b), making 'Ease In' and 'Ease Out' modes also linear in the current implementation — not cubic.

When Comp Duration is checked, repetitions are auto-calculated using a formula that references 'durationPerSet' before it is declared, which is a var-hoisting bug; the variable will be undefined at that point. The fixture does not exercise Comp Duration mode.
Custom-value keyframes (PropertyValueType.CUSTOM_VALUE) have their value stored as null and are skipped during setValueAtKey, matching the guard in the Keyframe constructor.
All keyframe property types are supported: spatial (TwoD_SPATIAL / ThreeD_SPATIAL), color, path (Mask Shape / Vector Shape), and standard numeric properties.
The script targets Adobe After Effects CS6 or later (ExtendScript / ES3 environment).
